text:
The track cycling is taking centre stage at theParis2024Olympicsat the Saint-Quentin-en-Yvelines velodrome.

And fans tuning into the action at home will have noticed a motorbike, called the derny, lapping around the track in the keirin,one of the blue ribband eventsin the Olympic cycling schedule.

But what exactly is the derny and what role does it play at the start of races?

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for updates from the Paris 2024 games

Here is everything you need to know.

The derny, which is electric, is used as a pacer.

The derny is used to bring riders up to a speed of 50 km per hour before the official start of the keirin race.

The derny bike completes three laps of the velodrome before leaving the track, at which point the race gets officially underway, with a three lap shootout to determine the winner of the race.

The positions of the riders behind the derny bike is determined by lots before the start, and competitors must remain behind it before it peels off.

Before 2015, the derny bikes used at major cycling competitions ran on petrol, producing a small sound as it lapped the track.

It takes its name from the French firm, Derny, who originally produced the bike.

The name is now applied to all small pacing bikes, regardless of the manufacturer.

30 riders are involved in the keirin, placed into five heats of six riders.

The top two automatically progress to the quarter-finals, with 12 advancing to the semi-finals and six taking part in the medal race.

Typically, riders will run the first two laps after the derny has peeled away as a pack, before attempting to break away on the final of the three laps.

However, competitors sometimes launch their attacks early, such as Jason Kenny, who started his attack as soon as the derny exited the track in the Tokyo 2020 final to claim the seventh gold medal of his illustrious career.

The keirin originated from Japan in 1948 as a betting event and it literally means ‘sports betting’ in Japanese.

The amount of money bet on the keirin in Japan is regularly around £4bn per year.

title: Why is there a motorbike on the track cycling at the Olympics? | Metro News
description: Find out what the derny bike is used for in the keirin race at the track cycling in the Paris 2024 Olympics.
